# Tableau MCQ Bank - PG Program in Data Analytics & Gen AI

## Quick Outline

Topics are taken from [prompt.txt](../prompt.txt). Topic links jump to the matching difficulty section.

- [Definition-Based Concept MCQs](#definition-concept-mcqs)
- [Beginner](#beginner-section) - [Core MCQs](#beginner-core) / [Scenario-Based Questions](#beginner-scenarios)
  Topics: [Tableau interface (Data pane, Shelves, Marks card)](#beginner-section); [Connecting to data sources](#beginner-section); [Dimensions vs Measures](#beginner-section); [Basic chart types (bar, line, pie, scatter)](#beginner-section); [Basic filters and sorting](#beginner-section); [Tableau Desktop vs Server vs Public vs Online](#beginner-section)
- [Intermediate](#intermediate-section) - [Core MCQs](#intermediate-core) / [Scenario-Based Questions](#intermediate-scenarios)
  Topics: [Calculated fields (basics)](#intermediate-section); [Table calculations](#intermediate-section); [Dual-axis and combo charts](#intermediate-section); [Basic geographic mapping](#intermediate-section); [Groups, sets, bins](#intermediate-section); [Parameters (basics)](#intermediate-section); [Dashboard layout and actions (filter/highlight actions)](#intermediate-section); [Joins vs data blending (basics)](#intermediate-section)
- [Advanced](#advanced-section) - [Core MCQs](#advanced-core) / [Scenario-Based Questions](#advanced-scenarios)
  Topics: [LOD (Level of Detail) expressions: FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE](#advanced-section); [Advanced/nested calculated fields](#advanced-section); [Advanced table calculations](#advanced-section); [Data blending vs joining vs relationships (data model)](#advanced-section); [Performance optimization (extracts vs live connections)](#advanced-section); [Story points and advanced dashboard storytelling](#advanced-section); [Tableau Server/Online publishing, permissions, scheduling](#advanced-section); [Dynamic parameter actions](#advanced-section); [Real-life: global sales performance dashboard](#advanced-section); [fixing slow workbooks with five joined tables](#advanced-section); [regional-manager filtered data access](#advanced-section)

<a id="definition-concept-mcqs"></a>
## Definition-Based Concept MCQs

#### Q1. Which definition best describes dimension? [Concept]

- **A.** A categorical field used to slice, group, or label data.
- **B.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** A categorical field used to slice, group, or label data. This definition helps analysts use dimension correctly in practical work.

#### Q2. Which definition best describes measure? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A numeric field that can be aggregated.
- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** A numeric field that can be aggregated. This definition helps analysts use measure correctly in practical work.

#### Q3. Which definition best describes mark? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** A visual representation of one or more data records in a view.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** A visual representation of one or more data records in a view. This definition helps analysts use mark correctly in practical work.

#### Q4. Which definition best describes shelf? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** A file export option used only for presentations.
- **D.** An area where fields are placed to define a view.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** An area where fields are placed to define a view. This definition helps analysts use shelf correctly in practical work.

#### Q5. Which definition best describes Marks card? [Concept]

- **A.** A control area for mark type, colour, size, label, detail, and tooltip.
- **B.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** A control area for mark type, colour, size, label, detail, and tooltip. This definition helps analysts use Marks card correctly in practical work.

#### Q6. Which definition best describes calculated field? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A field created from a formula or expression.
- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** A field created from a formula or expression. This definition helps analysts use calculated field correctly in practical work.

#### Q7. Which definition best describes parameter? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** A user-controlled value that can drive calculations, filters, or views.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** A user-controlled value that can drive calculations, filters, or views. This definition helps analysts use parameter correctly in practical work.

#### Q8. Which definition best describes LOD expression? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** A file export option used only for presentations.
- **D.** A calculation that controls the level of detail used for aggregation.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** A calculation that controls the level of detail used for aggregation. This definition helps analysts use LOD expression correctly in practical work.

#### Q9. Which definition best describes FIXED LOD? [Concept]

- **A.** An LOD expression that computes at specified dimensions regardless of the view.
- **B.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** An LOD expression that computes at specified dimensions regardless of the view. This definition helps analysts use FIXED LOD correctly in practical work.

#### Q10. Which definition best describes INCLUDE LOD? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** An LOD expression that adds dimensions to the calculation granularity.
- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** An LOD expression that adds dimensions to the calculation granularity. This definition helps analysts use INCLUDE LOD correctly in practical work.

#### Q11. Which definition best describes EXCLUDE LOD? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** An LOD expression that removes dimensions from the calculation granularity.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** An LOD expression that removes dimensions from the calculation granularity. This definition helps analysts use EXCLUDE LOD correctly in practical work.

- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** A file export option used only for presentations.
- **D.** A calculation performed on values already present in the view.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** A calculation performed on values already present in the view. This definition helps analysts use table calculation correctly in practical work.

- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** A prebuilt table calculation such as running total or percent of total. This definition helps analysts use quick table calculation correctly in practical work.

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** A file export option used only for presentations.
- **D.** A presentation-style step that explains a data narrative.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** A presentation-style step that explains a data narrative. This definition helps analysts use story point correctly in practical work.

#### Q17. Which definition best describes filter action? [Concept]

- **B.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** An interaction where selecting one view filters another view. This definition helps analysts use filter action correctly in practical work.

#### Q18. Which definition best describes highlight action? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** An interaction where selecting data highlights related marks.
- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** An interaction where selecting data highlights related marks. This definition helps analysts use highlight action correctly in practical work.

#### Q19. Which definition best describes parameter action? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** An interaction that changes a parameter value.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** An interaction that changes a parameter value. This definition helps analysts use parameter action correctly in practical work.

#### Q20. Which definition best describes set action? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** A file export option used only for presentations.
- **D.** An interaction that updates a set based on user selection.

**Explanation:** An interaction that updates a set based on user selection. This definition helps analysts use set action correctly in practical work.

- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.

- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** A connection that queries the source system directly.

**Explanation:** A connection that queries the source system directly. This definition helps analysts use live connection correctly in practical work.

#### Q24. Which definition best describes data blending? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** A file export option used only for presentations.
- **D.** Combining data from separate sources after aggregation.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** Combining data from separate sources after aggregation. This definition helps analysts use data blending correctly in practical work.

#### Q25. Which definition best describes relationship? [Concept]

- **A.** A logical connection between tables in Tableau data modelling.
- **B.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** A logical connection between tables in Tableau data modelling. This definition helps analysts use relationship correctly in practical work.

#### Q26. Which definition best describes join? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A row-level combination of tables based on matching fields.
- **C.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** A row-level combination of tables based on matching fields. This definition helps analysts use join correctly in practical work.

#### Q27. Which definition best describes union? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** A row-stacking operation that combines similar tables.
- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** A row-stacking operation that combines similar tables. This definition helps analysts use union correctly in practical work.

#### Q28. Which definition best describes group? [Concept]

- **A.** A dashboard colour setting with no analytical role.
- **B.** A server password used only for publishing.
- **C.** A file export option used only for presentations.
- **D.** A manual or logical collection of dimension members.

- **D.** A file export option used only for presentations.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** A grouping of continuous numeric values into intervals. This definition helps analysts use bin correctly in practical work.

<a id="beginner-section"></a>
## Beginner

<a id="beginner-core"></a>
### Core MCQs (Concept + Tool-Usage)

#### Q1. Which statement best explains the role of Tableau interface: Data pane, Shelves, Marks card in Tableau for UK data analytics work? [Concept]

- **A.** It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
- **B.** It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
- **C.** It guarantees that all source data is correct even when no validation is performed.
- **D.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** Tableau interface: Data pane, Shelves, Marks card matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q2. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of Tableau interface: Data pane, Shelves, Marks card in a workbook, worksheet, or dashboard? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
- **B.** Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard.
- **C.** Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.
- **D.** Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** Correct use of Tableau interface: Data pane, Shelves, Marks card means applying the right Tableau capability and checking it against marks, aggregations, filters, data source totals, permissions, and dashboard performance. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

#### Q3. What is the strongest reason to understand Connecting to data sources before presenting analysis? [Concept]

- **A.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.
- **B.** It should be used only after the report has already been published.
- **C.** It reduces ambiguity in definitions, calculations, and assumptions.
- **D.** It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** Connecting to data sources matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q4. Which workflow is the best way to use Connecting to data sources in production-style analysis? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.
- **B.** Build the answer manually and avoid leaving a repeatable calculation path.
- **C.** Hide errors in the final output without checking why they occurred.
- **D.** Start with the business requirement, use the correct feature, and test the output before sharing it.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** Correct use of Connecting to data sources means applying the right Tableau capability and checking it against marks, aggregations, filters, data source totals, permissions, and dashboard performance. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

#### Q5. How should Dimensions vs Measures be treated? [Concept]

- **A.** It supports repeatable analysis rather than one-off manual judgement.
- **B.** It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.
- **C.** It replaces the need to understand the business question.
- **D.** It is relevant only when the dataset contains fewer than one hundred rows.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** Dimensions vs Measures matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q6. How should Dimensions vs Measures be used so the result can be checked and repeated? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Hide errors in the final output without checking why they occurred.
- **B.** Create the output, inspect edge cases, and confirm that the result answers the stakeholder question.
- **C.** Prioritise cosmetic formatting before confirming the metric definition.
- **D.** Share the output immediately and wait for stakeholders to find any issues.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** Correct use of Dimensions vs Measures means applying the right Tableau capability and checking it against marks, aggregations, filters, data source totals, permissions, and dashboard performance. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

#### Q7. Which statement best connects Basic chart types: bar, line, pie, scatter to business decision-making? [Concept]

- **A.** It is relevant only when the dataset contains fewer than one hundred rows.
- **B.** It means the analyst no longer needs to document assumptions.
- **C.** It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
- **D.** It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** Basic chart types: bar, line, pie, scatter matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q8. What is the best operational habit when working with Basic chart types: bar, line, pie, scatter? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Share the output immediately and wait for stakeholders to find any issues.
- **B.** Paste static values or screenshots so the result cannot be refreshed or audited.
- **C.** Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
- **D.** Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** Correct use of Basic chart types: bar, line, pie, scatter means applying the right Tableau capability and checking it against marks, aggregations, filters, data source totals, permissions, and dashboard performance. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

#### Q9. Which statement best explains the role of Basic filters and sorting in Tableau for UK data analytics work? [Concept]

- **A.** It reduces ambiguity in definitions, calculations, and assumptions.
- **B.** It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
- **C.** It guarantees that all source data is correct even when no validation is performed.
- **D.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** Basic filters and sorting matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q10. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of Basic filters and sorting in a workbook, worksheet, or dashboard? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
- **B.** Start with the business requirement, use the correct feature, and test the output before sharing it.
- **C.** Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.
- **D.** Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** Correct use of Basic filters and sorting means applying the right Tableau capability and checking it against marks, aggregations, filters, data source totals, permissions, and dashboard performance. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

#### Q11. What is the strongest reason to understand Tableau Desktop vs Server vs Public vs Online before presenting analysis? [Concept]

- **B.** It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
- **C.** It guarantees that all source data is correct even when no validation is performed.
- **D.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** Basic filters and sorting matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q106. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of Basic filters and sorting in a workbook, worksheet, or dashboard? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
- **B.** Start with the business requirement, use the correct feature, and test the output before sharing it.
- **C.** Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.
- **D.** Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** Correct use of Basic filters and sorting means applying the right Tableau capability and checking it against marks, aggregations, filters, data source totals, permissions, and dashboard performance. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

<a id="beginner-scenarios"></a>
### Scenario-Based Questions - Beginner

#### Q1. You are working for a UK retail chain. A manager asks you to explain a small week-on-week KPI movement. The work involves Tableau interface: Data pane, Shelves, Marks card in Tableau. What should you do first? [Scenario]

- **A.** Delete outliers or missing values before checking what they represent.
- **B.** Clarify the metric, inspect the relevant fields, apply Tableau interface: Data pane, Shelves, Marks card in a controlled workbook, worksheet, or dashboard, and validate the result before sharing.
- **C.** Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
- **D.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Tableau interface: Data pane, Shelves, Marks card, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q2. A stakeholder at a London fintech team needs a quick but reliable answer to prepare a clean summary for a team meeting. The key skill is Connecting to data sources. What is the best next step? [Scenario]

- **A.** Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
- **B.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
- **C.** Check the business question first, use Connecting to data sources on the right data, and compare the output with a simple trusted total.
- **D.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Connecting to data sources, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q3. You receive a small data extract from an NHS reporting team and need to check why two simple totals do not match. How should you use Dimensions vs Measures? [Scenario]

- **A.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
- **B.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.
- **C.** Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
- **D.** Preserve the raw data, create a repeatable working view, and use Dimensions vs Measures only after checking basic quality issues.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Dimensions vs Measures, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q4. A non-technical colleague at a regional transport operator is unsure whether the output is trustworthy while you turn a raw export into a readable analyst note. Which approach is best? [Scenario]

- **A.** Ask what decision the output supports, apply Basic chart types: bar, line, pie, scatter, and explain any assumption in plain language.
- **B.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.
- **C.** Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
- **D.** Assume another team has already validated the extract and skip reconciliation.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Basic chart types: bar, line, pie, scatter, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q5. You are working for a local council analytics team. A manager asks you to spot the main category driving a change in performance. The work involves Basic filters and sorting in Tableau. What should you do first? [Scenario]

- **A.** Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
- **B.** Clarify the metric, inspect the relevant fields, apply Basic filters and sorting in a controlled workbook, worksheet, or dashboard, and validate the result before sharing.
- **C.** Assume another team has already validated the extract and skip reconciliation.
- **D.** Hide caveats so the final answer looks more confident.

<a id="intermediate-section"></a>
## Intermediate

<a id="intermediate-core"></a>
### Core MCQs (Concept + Tool-Usage)

#### Q1. Which statement best explains the role of Calculated fields basics in Tableau for UK data analytics work? [Concept]

- **A.** It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
- **B.** It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
- **C.** It guarantees that all source data is correct even when no validation is performed.
- **D.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** Calculated fields basics matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q2. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of Calculated fields basics in a workbook, worksheet, or dashboard? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
- **B.** Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard.
- **C.** Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.
- **D.** Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** Correct use of Calculated fields basics means applying the right Tableau capability and checking it against marks, aggregations, filters, data source totals, permissions, and dashboard performance. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

#### Q3. What is the strongest reason to understand Table calculations before presenting analysis? [Concept]

- **A.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.
- **B.** It should be used only after the report has already been published.
- **C.** It reduces ambiguity in definitions, calculations, and assumptions.
- **D.** It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** Table calculations matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

<a id="intermediate-scenarios"></a>
### Scenario-Based Questions - Intermediate

#### Q1. You are supporting a UK retail chain and must prepare a monthly performance pack for finance and operations. Several teams disagree about the numbers, and Calculated fields basics is central. What should you do? [Scenario]

- **A.** Delete outliers or missing values before checking what they represent.
- **B.** Agree definitions, profile the source data, build a repeatable solution using Calculated fields basics, validate against source totals, and document caveats.
- **C.** Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
- **D.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Calculated fields basics, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q2. A refreshed deliverable for a London fintech team looks wrong while you compare regional performance where teams use different definitions. The investigation involves Table calculations. What is the strongest approach? [Scenario]

- **A.** Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
- **B.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
- **C.** Trace the data grain, test the transformation or calculation path, use Table calculations deliberately, and review the result with the data owner.
- **D.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Table calculations, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q3. Your manager at an NHS reporting team asks you to combine multiple extracts before a senior stakeholder review with a repeatable process. How should you apply Dual-axis and combo charts? [Scenario]

- **A.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
- **B.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.
- **C.** Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
- **D.** Create a reproducible workflow, check edge cases, validate the output, and make the assumptions visible to stakeholders.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Dual-axis and combo charts, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q4. You inherit a partially built workbook, worksheet, or dashboard for a regional transport operator while trying to investigate why a refreshed dashboard shows unexpected totals. The weak area is Basic geographic mapping. What is the best response? [Scenario]

- **A.** Compare the current result with a trusted baseline, isolate where Basic geographic mapping affects the numbers, and fix the logic transparently.
- **B.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.
- **C.** Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
- **D.** Assume another team has already validated the extract and skip reconciliation.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Basic geographic mapping, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q5. You are supporting a local council analytics team and must standardise a repeatable reporting process across departments. Several teams disagree about the numbers, and Groups, sets, bins is central. What should you do? [Scenario]

- **A.** Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
- **B.** Agree definitions, profile the source data, build a repeatable solution using Groups, sets, bins, validate against source totals, and document caveats.
- **C.** Assume another team has already validated the extract and skip reconciliation.
- **D.** Hide caveats so the final answer looks more confident.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Groups, sets, bins, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

- **B.** Hide caveats so the final answer looks more confident.

- **B.** Agree definitions, profile the source data, build a repeatable solution using Calculated fields basics, validate against source totals, and document caveats.
- **C.** Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
- **D.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Calculated fields basics, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

<a id="advanced-section"></a>
## Advanced

<a id="advanced-core"></a>
### Core MCQs (Concept + Tool-Usage)

#### Q1. Which statement best explains the role of LOD expressions: FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE in Tableau for UK data analytics work? [Concept]

- **A.** It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
- **B.** It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
- **C.** It guarantees that all source data is correct even when no validation is performed.
- **D.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** LOD expressions: FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q2. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of LOD expressions: FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE in a workbook, worksheet, or dashboard? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
- **B.** Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard.
- **C.** Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.
- **D.** Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** Correct use of LOD expressions: FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE means applying the right Tableau capability and checking it against marks, aggregations, filters, data source totals, permissions, and dashboard performance. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

#### Q3. What is the strongest reason to understand Advanced and nested calculated fields before presenting analysis? [Concept]

- **A.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.
- **B.** It should be used only after the report has already been published.
- **C.** It reduces ambiguity in definitions, calculations, and assumptions.
- **D.** It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** Advanced and nested calculated fields matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q4. Which workflow is the best way to use Advanced and nested calculated fields in production-style analysis? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.
- **B.** Build the answer manually and avoid leaving a repeatable calculation path.
- **C.** Hide errors in the final output without checking why they occurred.
- **D.** Start with the business requirement, use the correct feature, and test the output before sharing it.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** Correct use of Advanced and nested calculated fields means applying the right Tableau capability and checking it against marks, aggregations, filters, data source totals, permissions, and dashboard performance. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

#### Q5. How should Advanced table calculations be treated? [Concept]

- **A.** It supports repeatable analysis rather than one-off manual judgement.
- **B.** It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.
- **C.** It replaces the need to understand the business question.
- **D.** It is relevant only when the dataset contains fewer than one hundred rows.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** Advanced table calculations matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the workbook, worksheet, or dashboard. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q6. How should Advanced table calculations be used so the result can be checked and repeated? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Hide errors in the final output without checking why they occurred.
- **B.** Create the output, inspect edge cases, and confirm that the result answers the stakeholder question.
- **C.** Prioritise cosmetic formatting before confirming the metric definition.
- **D.** Share the output immediately and wait for stakeholders to find any issues.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** Correct use of Advanced table calculations means applying the right Tableau capability and checking it against marks, aggregations, filters, data source totals, permissions, and dashboard performance. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

- **B.** It means the analyst no longer needs to document assumptions.

<a id="advanced-scenarios"></a>
### Scenario-Based Questions - Advanced (complex, multi-step, case-study style)

#### Q1. You lead an analytics workstream for a UK retail chain and must design an auditable executive reporting workflow under a tight deadline. The solution depends on LOD expressions: FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE in Tableau. What is the best approach? [Scenario]

- **A.** Delete outliers or missing values before checking what they represent.
- **B.** Map requirements and data grain, design the solution around LOD expressions: FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE, test security and performance, validate with owners, and publish with monitoring.
- **C.** Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
- **D.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of LOD expressions: FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q2. A board-facing workbook, worksheet, or dashboard for a London fintech team has disputed totals while you resolve disputed board-level numbers across several systems. Advanced and nested calculated fields is one of the main levers. What should you do? [Scenario]

- **A.** Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
- **B.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
- **C.** Separate definition issues from tooling issues, use Advanced and nested calculated fields in a governed workflow, test edge cases, and document the decision trail.
- **D.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Advanced and nested calculated fields, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q3. A production analytics process at an NHS reporting team must support audit, governance, and performance while you improve a slow production analytics process without changing the business definition. How should you handle Advanced table calculations? [Scenario]

- **A.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
- **B.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.
- **C.** Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
- **D.** Build an auditable solution, optimise only after correctness is proven, and agree sign-off criteria with business and technical owners.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Advanced table calculations, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.
